title: Kaworu Nagisa The most creative villain of all time
display_title: Kaworu Nagisa The most creative villain of all time
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu Nagisa: The most creative villain of all time. Post by DonZabu » Sun Nov 16, 2008 11:54 pm Ok, we all know that, at the end of episode 24, Kaworu has Shinji destroy him so that humans may have a chance at living. On the surface, this may seem like a heroic move. But look deeper: that one 'noble sacrifice' completely destroyed what remained of Shinji's psyche, sending him so deeply into depression that two whole episodes are devoted to Shinji grappling with his inner demons. Could Kaworu's 'heroic sacrifice' have been a deliberate attack on Shinji using reverse psychology?

[]: Post by Deluxe » Sun Nov 16, 2008 11:57 pm Just like Legato from Trigun.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:04 am So exactly what's in this for Kaworu? Aside from the joy of being a secretively evil silver-haired bishounen? (And the topic title is a little misleading.)

[]: Post by Sachi » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:07 am BRILLIANT! SEELE wanted Shinji's psyche to be weak so he'd agree with instrumentality anyways. But what about Kaworu trying to help Shinji deal with reality in EoE?

[]: Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:08 am If Kaworu's "betrayal" was a deliberate ruse to get Shinji all sad, then Seele wouldn't have been talking about how they made a mistake.

[]: Post by Formless One » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:10 am Wasn't this the logic that That Dude in the Suede used to justify putting Kaworu on his top 11 creepiest anime villains of all time list? Of course, he also got the spot because effected the audience's enjoyment of the end of the series, but yeah.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:26 am Sachi_13 wrote: BRILLIANT! SEELE wanted Shinji's psyche to be weak so he'd agree with instrumentality anyways.

[]: Post by DonZabu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:46 am Formless One wrote: Wasn't this the logic that That Dude in the Suede used to justify putting Kaworu on his top 11 creepiest anime villains of all time list? Of course, he also got the spot because effected the audience's enjoyment of the end of the series, but yeah.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 12:53 am The proposal isn't a hard one to come up with; I've seen it appear a fair number of times. Actually, the longer you hang out at these places, the more variations upon the "person A did ~ just to fuck person B up" conspiracy theories you'll bear witness to. Gendo's another popular target for the role of person A. I don't give much credence to these ideas; they never seem to withstand scrutiny very well. Did I mention that this thread has an awful title?

[]: Post by V » Mon Nov 17, 2008 1:37 am no Kaworu wasn't trying to hurt Shinji by his own death no, Kaworu is not a villain

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 2:28 am NO! I WON'T BELIEVE THAT KAWORU-KUN IS A VILLAIN! IT'S NOT POSSIBLE! IT'S A LIE!!!! :slams fist:

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 17, 2008 2:52 am He wasn't being a villain, we proved that, but he's definitely not a hero or a martyr. He did it for the lulz.

[]: Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Nov 17, 2008 3:28 am Yeah, I remember that one scene where he rigged a couple of ferries with explosives and told the passengers of each ferry that they held the controls to blow up the other ferry. Then, if they wanted to live, they had to blow up the other ferry, and if one of the ferries didn't go boom by midnight, he'd blow them both up. Good times, good times.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 17, 2008 7:42 am I guess there isn't much to discuss here after all.
